frame_analyses.description: Setup view showing Penrose drain on gauze with instruments positioned. Title screen visible.
Instruments visible, beginning to manipulate suture material
Loading needle with suture, instruments working to secure needle in needle holder
Placing suture through first mark on Penrose drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture placement appears close to intended mark location

frame_analyses.description: Continuing suture placement through drain tissue
Beginning first throw of surgeon's knot, creating initial loop
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appears to be forming proper surgeon's knot with double wrap

frame_analyses.description: Tightening first throw, surgeon's knot formation visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good knot tightening technique, proper surgeon's knot structure

frame_analyses.description: Beginning second throw with single wrap, switching hand dominance
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand switching demonstrated for second throw

frame_analyses.description: Performing third and final throw with single wrap
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Completing knot sequence with appropriate hand switching
Cutting both ends appropriately close to knot

frame_analyses.description: Continuing to cut second suture end
Task completed, final view of secured drain with knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final result shows good closure of drain slit

knot_assessments.notes: Proper surgeon's knot with double wrap clearly visible
Single throw with proper hand switching
Final single throw with hand switching, knot appears secure

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain slit appears well closed with no visible gaps after knot tightening

technique_summary: Excellent execution of intracorporeal suturing with proper surgeon's knot followed by correct hand switching sequence. Clean technique throughout with good final result.
improvement_suggestions: Consider slightly more precise suture placement to minimize deviation from marks
